content.full_text:
A-26-3149

August 5, 1976

Ms. Lita A. Smith
21813 Devlin
Hawaiian Gardens, California 90716

Dear Ms. Smith:

This letter is in response to your inquiry concerning your filing
obligations under the Political Reform Act.

You have stated in your letter that your group plans to circulate
petitions in an attempt to qualify a recall measure for the ballot.
You anticipate all circulators to be nonpaid and very little money
to be spent in your efforts to have the measure place on the ballot.

The law specifically addresses this situation in Section 84202 of
the Political Reform Act which states:

"84202(a) Not later than sixty-five days after a measure
has been qualified for the ballot, the proponent shall
file a campaign statement, the closing date of which shall
be the fifty-eighth day following the qualification of the
measure.

(b) If any proposed measures does not qualify for the bal-
lot, the proponent shall file a campaign statement within
sixty-five days after the final deadline for circulating
the petition, the closing date of which shall be the fifty-
eighth day following the deadline."

Accordingly, your group will be required to file one report whether
or not your measure qualifies for the ballot, which includes all
money raised and spent in connection with the circulation drive.
The report must be filed regardless of how much money is involved.

If it is a city measure, you will file with the city clerk; a county
measure with the county clerk. I have enclosed some campaign state-
ments for your use, please choose the one that best suits your group.

Let me know if you desire additional information and I will be happy
to assist you. Our Los Angeles phone number is 620-5196.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw bcc: Jeanne P.

American Studies,
C.U. 90716
August 3, 1976

Dear Political Practices Committee:
Technical Assistance Analysts Inc.,
1100 Bay Street
Lacamonte, CA 95814

Dear Committee, May I concern

We are contemplating a
recall movement.
Then, unless months we
expect to raise will be tolower
legal advertisement issued by
late and the printing of the
petitions forms. We hope to
raise the monies of the base
of small donations at coffee
hours etc.

Signature gathering will be
on a purely, Volun-[illegible] basis.
Please let me know what
the mailing requirements are
so that we can become aligned
with government codes in
that respect.

The expenses will early be
a good deal under $500.01.
After the short campaign are
committed i will closed to out.
Your most excellent help
would
[end of page]

will be greatly appreciated.
Respectfully,
[illegible] Newspaper
Attn: F. L. Smith
2158 Nevlyn
Shumean Parkway
CA - 90716

sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing obligations under the Political Reform Act for a group planning to circulate petitions for a recall measure with nonpaid circulators and minimal spending?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The group must file one campaign statement regardless of whether the measure qualifies for the ballot, within 65 days after qualification (or final petition deadline if it does not qualify), including all money raised and spent, with filing location determined by whether it is a city or county measure.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal Q/C section headers present. Question and conclusion extracted from letter body content. Document clearly structured as FPPC response letter with legal citation and specific filing guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the filing obligations under the Political Reform Act for a group planning to circulate petitions for a recall measure with nonpaid circulators and minimal spending?

CONCLUSION: The group must file one campaign statement regardless of whether the measure qualifies for the ballot, within 65 days after qualification (or final petition deadline if it does not qualify), including all money raised and spent, with filing location determined by whether it is a city or county measure.
